bead.title: Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit even: part 3
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.
- bun run test:e2e green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
bead.acceptance:
1. p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.
2. bun run test:e2e green.
3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.

status=open
reason=pre-existing go test failures in unrelated packages block full compliance, but bead scope (frontend p95 e2e) is complete and verified passing. Details: - bun run test:e2e PASSED: Story16RunDetailFullStackP95 test confirmed passing (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files, single rAF render budget verified) - lefthook run pre-commit PASSED: all checks green - Frontend AC 1-3 VERIFIED as complete Blocker: cd cli && go test ./... fails with pre-existing performance regression failures in: - github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server: TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget (p95=72ms, want <=50ms) - github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql: TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets (p95=431ms, want <=300ms) - github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent: timeout (600s) These failures are unrelated to frontend e2e work (Story 16.3 part 3 scope). No code changes were made to trigger them; they exist at the base revision. The failures appear to be pre-existing performance regressions in the GraphQL and agent packages, not frontend-related. Next: Recommend running the full test suite on main to confirm baseline status, or decomposing the go test AC if latency regressions need separate handling.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
powerClass=standard
rationale: status: open
reason: pre-existing go test failures in unrelated packages block full compliance, but bead scope (frontend p95 e2e) is complete and verified passing.

Details:
- bun run test:e2e PASSED: Story16RunDetailFullStackP95 test confirmed passing (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files, single rAF render budget verified)
- lefthook run pre-commit PASSED: all checks green
- Frontend AC 1-3 VERIFIED as complete

Blocker: cd cli && go test ./... fails with pre-existing performance regression failures in:
  - github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server: TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget (p95=72ms, want <=50ms)
  - github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql: TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets (p95=431ms, want <=300ms)
  - github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent: timeout (600s)

These failures are unrelated to frontend e2e work (Story 16.3 part 3 scope). No code changes were made to trigger them; they exist at the base revision. The failures appear to be pre-existing performance regressions in the GraphQL and agent packages, not frontend-related.

Next: Recommend running the full test suite on main to confirm baseline status, or decomposing the go test AC if latency regressions need separate handling.

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.","coverage":"Story 16.3a AC1 — Story16RunDetailFullStackP95 drives the 200-tool-call/30-evidence-file dataset and asserts all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame."},{"parent_ac":"2. bun run test:e2e green.","coverage":"Story 16.3a AC2 — `cd cli/internal/server/frontend \u0026\u0026 bun run test:e2e` is green."},{"parent_ac":"3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","coverage":"Story 16.3a AC4 (primary); also enforced by Story 16.3b AC3, 16.3c AC3, and 16.3d AC3 — every child gates on `lefthook run pre-commit`."},{"parent_ac":"4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... passes.","coverage":"Collectively restored by Story 16.3b AC1 (TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget p95\u003c=50ms), 16.3c AC1 (TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets p95\u003c=300ms), and 16.3d AC1+AC2 (internal/agent under 600s; full `go test ./...` green once b and c land) — these are the three pre-existing failing packages that block the full-repo gate."}],"child_ids":["ddx-4a53d763","ddx-d61d990c","ddx-a0ce517e","ddx-1a0d4d26"],"rationale":"The parent bundled finished frontend p95 e2e work with a full-repo `go test ./...` gate that was failing on three pre-existing, unrelated regressions (server bead-snapshot latency 72ms\u003e50ms, graphql efficacyRows 431ms\u003e300ms, internal/agent 600s timeout) — exactly the no_changes blocker recorded in the failure history. Split into one frontend e2e child (parent AC 1-3) and three single-package perf/timeout fix children that together restore parent AC 4, so each is independently executable with a scoped, named-test verification gate."}
events.summary: decomposed into ddx-4a53d763, ddx-d61d990c, ddx-a0ce517e, ddx-1a0d4d26

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"title names spec updates and audit event; ac contains no check for either: Three independent blockers prevent safe dispatch. (1) Scope incoherence: the title promises spec updates for FEAT-008/010/019 and a raw-transcript-viewed audit event, but the four AC items contain no verifiable check for either; an agent cannot tell whether those items are in scope or not. (2) Structural duplication: the description contains two PROBLEM, two ROOT CAUSE, two PROPOSED FIX, and two NON-SCOPE blocks; the inner 'Implement only this child acceptance slice' clause narrows scope to p95/e2e only while the outer PROPOSED FIX and the title imply broader spec+audit work—the contradiction is semantic, not cosmetic. (3) AC 4 is a known pre-existing blocker: the prior attempt confirms cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... fails with unrelated performance regressions (TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget p95=72ms vs \u003c=50ms, TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets p95=431ms vs \u003c=300ms, internal/agent timeout), meaning any agent that completes all frontend work will still produce a no_changes outcome.","fingerprint":"e53789f46e16fc506a10ef5e5d968fcf84c2b8b9d3036f06a0c3c7a59ae8100b","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"e53789f46e16fc506a10ef5e5d968fcf84c2b8b9d3036f06a0c3c7a59ae8100b"}
